label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what the passage mainly talks about, with options A to D. I need to figure out which one is the correct answer. First, I'll read through the passage again to get the main idea. The passage starts by talking about how both women and men are surrounded by media about dieting, exercise, fashion, and hairstyles. It mentions that men used to just need a nice suit to be considered smart and handsome, but things have changed. Then there's a survey by UWE saying 80% of men are unhappy with their bodies, specifically about beer bellies and lack of muscles. The text discusses how role models in TV and magazines influence people's perception of a perfect body. It also mentions a product like a muscle-enhancing shirt as a gimmick, and Joe Warner's opinion that people should exercise instead of taking shortcuts. The conclusion is that exercise is good, but beauty isn't everything, and character matters more. The author ends by saying they might go to the gym to look healthier and improve confidence. Now, looking at the options: A) You shouldn't judge a book by its cover. – This is a moral or lesson mentioned in the passage ("we mustn't forget that beauty is only skin deep. Character is more important than appearance.") but is that the main topic? The passage is more about the trend of men worrying about their body image rather than the lesson about judging by appearance. So maybe A is a supporting point but not the main topic. B) More and more people become obese in England. – The passage mentions obesity but it's part of the discussion about why men are concerned with their bodies. However, the main focus isn't on the increase in obesity but the increased consciousness about body image. So B might not be correct. C) Role models put great pressure on common people. – The text does mention role models as a possible cause for the pressure, but that's one of the points discussed, not the overall main idea. The main idea is broader than just the pressure from role models. D) Men become more and more conscious of their body image. – The first paragraph states that the days when men only needed a suit are gone, and the survey shows 80% of men are unhappy with their bodies. The rest of the passage discusses reasons and reactions to this trend. So D
